dimensions.rationale: Structure and flow are solid but two transitions need bridging.
Two critical policy conflicts found; addressable with rewording.
Speed and uptime claims need benchmark citations.
Opening hook and model specificity are strong trust signals.

priority_fixes: Fix the 'fully automated' claim — it directly contradicts Enterprise Data Policy §3.2.
Anchor the '3× faster' metric to a named competitor and public benchmark.
Qualify the privacy claim: add 'by default' to cover the opt-in cloud sync.
Add a bridge sentence between the problem slide and the demo.

findings.title: Strong opening hook
findings.detail: The opening anecdote about a failed product demo was vivid and relatable. It established stakes immediately.

findings.title: Abrupt transition after problem statement
findings.detail: The transition from the problem slide to the demo felt rushed. There was no bridge sentence to orient the audience.
findings.suggestion: Add a one-sentence recap: 'That's the problem — here's how PitchPilot solves it.'

findings.title: Solution slide overloaded with jargon
findings.detail: Slide 3 uses 'multi-agent orchestration', 'LoRA fine-tuning', and 'tokenised function dispatch' without explanation.
findings.suggestion: Lead with the outcome ('analyzes your pitch in 90 seconds') before explaining the mechanism.

findings.title: Speed metric lacks benchmark context
findings.detail: '3× faster' is a compelling claim but the baseline is never stated. Sophisticated audiences will dismiss unanchored comparisons.
findings.suggestion: Name the competitor and link to a reproducible benchmark. E.g. 'vs. GPT-4o on the MLPerf inference suite'.

findings.title: 'Fully automated' conflicts with policy §3.2
findings.detail: Your enterprise data-handling policy (section 3.2) requires human review for model outputs above a confidence threshold of 0.95.
findings.suggestion: Rephrase to: 'Automated with optional human-in-the-loop review for high-stakes decisions.'
findings.policy_reference: Enterprise Data Policy §3.2 — Human Oversight Requirement

findings.title: 99.9 % uptime SLA not reflected in current contract
findings.detail: The standard enterprise contract offers 99.5 % SLA. Promising 99.9 % during a pitch creates a potential contractual liability.
findings.suggestion: Either reference the premium-tier SLA or say 'up to 99.9 %' with a footnote.
findings.policy_reference: SLA Addendum v2 — Enterprise Standard Tier

findings.title: 'Nothing leaves your network' needs qualification
findings.detail: Architecture slide 8 shows an optional cloud-sync feature. The blanket privacy claim may be technically false for customers who enable it.
findings.suggestion: Add 'by default' and mention the opt-in cloud sync explicitly.

findings.title: Skeptical Investor: differentiation is unclear
findings.detail: A skeptical investor would immediately ask how this differs from a well-prompted ChatGPT plus screen recording.
findings.suggestion: Lead with the on-device / privacy differentiator earlier and repeat it at close.

findings.title: Technical Reviewer: model card details appreciated
findings.detail: The Technical Reviewer persona found the mention of specific model names (Gemma 3n, FunctionGemma) credible and reassuring.

findings.title: Compliance Officer: data retention policy missing
findings.detail: No mention of how long rehearsal recordings are retained locally. A Compliance Officer would flag this immediately.
findings.suggestion: Add one bullet on local-only storage, auto-deletion policy, and no cloud upload.

persona_questions.question: How is this different from asking ChatGPT to review my slide deck?
persona_questions.follow_up: And if the answer is 'on-device', why can't a compliance-aware wrapper around GPT-4o do the same thing?

persona_questions.question: What does '3× faster' mean, and is there a published benchmark?
Your slides say 'no data leaves the device' but slide 8 shows a cloud sync icon — can you clarify?
Has your automated decision pipeline been reviewed against GDPR Article 22?
What happens when Gemma 3n hallucinates during OCR — is there a confidence threshold?

claims.text: Our platform is fully automated — no manual review required.
We achieve 99.9 % uptime across all enterprise tiers.
All customer data is stored exclusively on-device — nothing leaves your network.
We outperform every competitor by 3× on inference speed.

summary: Overall readiness is 72/100. The pitch has a strong hook and credible technical specificity, but two compliance conflicts need resolution before presenting to an enterprise buyer. The privacy and automation claims are the highest-risk items. Prepare for the ChatGPT differentiation question — it will come from every audience.
